curation.synopsis: Standalone Imperial-Guard-anchored 40K novel set on the jungle-and-swamp death-world of Phaedra, where the Imperium has been bogged down in a decade-long stalemate against a T'au expansion force backed by local secessionist insurgents. The 19th Arkan Confederates — a fresh regiment still haunted by the trauma of their home world's recent rebellion — are deployed to break the deadlock, but command sends them in piecemeal and the regiment scatters under fire. Dual Imperial POV across the campaign: Commissar Holt Iverson, who has spent so long on Phaedra that his sense of duty has corroded into private vendetta, sees in the Confederates a last chance at redemption; Colonel Ensor Cutler holds the regimental command line as the campaign disintegrates. Phaedra itself functions as an antagonist — a Heart-of-Darkness / Apocalypse-Now register where the warp leaks through the canopy, ghosts of past Imperial atrocities surface, and Tau Empire infiltrators move through the trees. Opens Fehervari's loose 'Dark Coil' continuity: weird-40K standalones that share an undercurrent of cosmic-horror taint without explicit cross-references.
